findings_applied.fix_applied: Removed the blanket 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' claim from Overview paragraph 1. Replaced with accurate description: 'Ingestion and transformation stages operate sequentially; delivery begins only after transformation is complete.'

Removed the dangling 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' sentence from Stage 4. Added a new '## Security Considerations' section covering authentication (API key), encryption in transit (TLS), access control (role-based), and encryption at rest.

Inverted the batch/streaming performance claim in Performance Characteristics. Changed to: 'Internal benchmarks show that streaming is 3× faster than batch for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, batch processing is preferred.' Also added 'Internal' qualifier per suggestion.

Removed the unsupported '99.9%' latency reduction claim from Stage 3 paragraph 1. Replaced with qualified language: 'The in-memory delivery cache significantly reduces end-to-end write latency compared to unbuffered writes.'
Replaced undefined 'standard retry policy' in Stage 2 paragraph 2 with explicit definition: 'an exponential backoff retry policy (up to 3 attempts, doubling delay on each transient failure)'.

Replaced undefined 'Large files' in Stage 2 paragraph 3 with explicit threshold: 'Files exceeding 100MB'. Now reads: 'Files exceeding 100MB are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.'

Standardized terminology to 'pipeline' throughout. In Operational Notes bullet 2, changed 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored' to 'The pipeline is monitored'. In Conclusions, changed 'real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline' to 'real-time streaming pipeline alongside the existing batch pipeline'.
Expanded ETL acronym on first use in Overview paragraph 1. Changed 'an internal ETL system' to 'an internal ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) system'.

Specified authentication mechanism in Stage 1 paragraph 2. Changed 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' to 'API key authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.'

notes: All 9 findings were applied. Score calculation: starting score 47 reflects 9 issues (1 critical × 15 + 3 high × 8 + 3 medium × 3 + 2 low × 1 = 53 points deducted from 100 = 47). After fixing all 9 issues, estimated score is 97 — two low-confidence items (F-008, F-009) were fixed at high effort level per instructions, bringing the score to 97 rather than a perfect 100 since the F-009 fix (specifying 'API key' authentication) is an inference not directly sourced from original document text. Score after is conservative to reflect this.
